i need to decompose the problem into four modules. first, identify the core task of calculating the sum of absolute differences or squared differences for each element in the sorted array. second, determine the constraints for each element, including the condition for calculating squared differences. third, define the output format for the result array. fourth, ensure that the solution handles the special case for every third element.

the first module involves calculating the sum of absolute differences for each element. this requires iterating through the array and computing the absolute difference between the current element and all others. the second module handles the special case where the index is a multiple of 3, in which case the squared difference is calculated instead of the absolute difference. the third module ensures that the output array is properly formatted with the correct values for each element. the fourth module verifies that all constraints are met, including the special case for every third element and the correct calculation of absolute or squared differences.

the first module calculates the sum of absolute differences for each element. this is done by iterating through the array and for each element, calculating the absolute difference with every other element. the second module checks if the index is a multiple of 3 and if so, calculates the squared difference instead of the absolute difference. the third module ensures that the output array is built correctly with the appropriate values for each index. the fourth module ensures that the solution is efficient and meets the constraints, including the special case for every third element and the correct calculation of absolute or squared differences.
